title: The Heron
lines: A Heron stood by a lake, looking for his dinner.
Small fish swam near, but he disdained them.
As the day went on and he caught nothing, he grew hungrier.
At last he saw a small fish and tried to catch it, but it escaped.
As the sun set, he realized he would go hungry.
'I should have been satisfied with the small fish," he lamented.
moral: Those who are too greedy often end up with nothing.
